methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Hystar is a Series B climate and energy company that has raised $62M in equity plus €26M from the EU Innovation Fund. The valuation analysis triangulates across a universe of 9 comparable companies in the same sector and stage: 1 public company ( Linza, ticker LNZA) and 8 private peers including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis used the $62M total raised as a proxy base valuation and applied a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $118M. Peer-median analysis across the 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables produced a median valuation of $355M, with a range from $120M to $5.0B reflecting substantial dispersion within the peer set, notably driven by Commonwealth Fusion Systems' $5.0B valuation. 

The triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $100M to $408M, with a median implied value of $236M. The wide dispersion between the stage-progression floor and the peer-median estimate reflects material uncertainty around technology maturity, capital efficiency, and market timing relative to comparables. The peer range itself ($120M–$5.0B) underscores sector volatility. Key sensitivities include progress toward commercialization milestones and deployment velocity relative to peer cohorts.
